text: that revealed a tumor which was confined to the right lower lobe bronchus.
As seen here, the middle lobe and the tertiary carina are free of tumor.
The surgery begins with division of the inferior pulmonary ligament, clearing the nodes and identification of the inferior pulmonary vein.
To help in looping of the inferior pulmonary vein, the posterior pleural cut is taken that begins at the subcarinal region and it is extended proximally.
This aids not only in looping of the inferior pulmonary vein, also helps in better nodal dissection and also identification of the main bronchus.
This is extended proximally up to the acai gospel.
Along with this, the posterior fissural pleura is also divided and this brings the right main bronchus into better view.
Once this is done, the anterior pulmonary vein is looped using a straight dissector and adequate space is created.
The surgery proceeds with the clearing of the additions between the upper and the lower lobe and identification of the interlobar PA in the fissure.
Once identified, the fissure is further developed anteriorly and posteriorly.

One has to be careful of the arterial anatomy to avoid any anomalous branches to the middle lobes and in this case there were no any anomalous branches and hence the anterior and posterior fissures were developed using a power stapling device.

This division brings the pulmonary artery into full view as seen here and the straight resector which is an important tool of a VAT surgeon is used to loop the pulmonary artery and space is created beneath the pulmonary artery to pass a powered staple device.
The interlobar nodes which are N1 nodes are also cleared which is part of oncological clearance as well also it entails better application of the stapler.
The division of the inferior pulmonary vein follows suit, also using a powered stapling device.

Prior to division of the bronchus, it will be prudent to proceed with the subcarinal and the parasophageal nodal dissection as the specimen will help in retracting the bronchus for better clearance of the nodal stations.

In this case, in view of the significant peribronchial tumor extension, the bronchotomy was made quite proximally close to the middle lobe bronchus to avoid peribronchial margin being positive for tumor.
While dividing the bronchus, one encounters the branches from the bronchial artery which can be tackled using an energy device.
After division of the bronchus, the specimen is retrieved using a specimen retrieval bag and it is sent for the frozen section.
While awaiting the frozen section, a template mediastinal lymph node dissection is done of the paratracheal region.
One has to be careful to preserve the phrenic as well as the vagus nerve while proceeding with the paratracheal dissection superior to the acagas.
The frozen ripples were suggestive of a free bronchial and peribronchial margins.
Hence, the bronchotomy was closed in an intermittent fashion using a PDS suture material.
However, post closure, the middle lobe was not getting adequately ventilated.
Hence, the lower sutures of the bronchotomy was opened and the middle lobe bronchus was identified using an on-table bronchoscope.
And under guidance, the bronchotomy was closed again to ensure patency of the middle lobe bronchus.
However, even with this exercise, it was futile as the middle lobe was not getting ventilated.
Hence, a decision was made to proceed with the WADS bronchoplasty of the middle lobe bronchus.
The bronchotomy was opened, the bronchus was looped and the middle lobe bronchus was disconnected from the bronchus intermedius.
The bronchus intermedius is revised and mobilized to ensure tension-free anastomosis.
But this procedure brought in with it a peculiar problem that will be seen further.
The divided knuckle of the pulmonary artery comes in between the bronchus intermedius and the middle lobe bronchus.
The 4-0 proline with the double needle was used for suturing.
One can see the divided knuckle of the pulmonary artery between the intermedius and the middle lobe bronchus.
Hence, the middle lobe bronchus was hitched against the chest wall.
The other end of the 4-0 proline is used to fashion the posterior layer of the anastomosis.
The surgeon uses a suction cannula to depress the divided end of the pulmonary artery and the needle holder is used to boast to throw and retrieve the needle from the bronchus intermedius while
A curved dissector helps the surgeon in doing the same in the bronchus of the middle lobe.

The surgeon is seen maneuvering the needle as well as the thread over the knuckle of the pulmonary artery without injuring it and is seen taking throws at equal distance on both the bronchial cut ends.
Once the posterior layer is over, both the bronchus are approximated but not completely tightened.
This maneuver displaces the knuckle of the pulmonary artery away from the anastomotic field.
The other end of the suture is now used to proceed with the anterior anastomosis.
The surgeon now ensures that the sutures are not entangled with the posterior layer and also he takes sutures at equidistance without any knotting of the sutures.
The instrument of choice for suturing is up to the surgeon's comfort.
Here the surgeon is seen using a needle driver, a curve dissector as well as a scanlon forceps to aid in efficient suturing.
This suturing is continued till the superior edge of the middle lobe and the bronchus intermedius.
Once the superior edge is reached, the posterior layers needle is brought inside out on the bronchus intermedius.
Attempt at ventilating the upper and middle lobe are done.
One can see air escaping through the
anastomotic side but one can also appreciate the middle lobe bronchus partially getting ventilated.

Hence to tighten the anastomosis a nerve hook is used and it is tightened cautiously to avoid snapping of the anastomotic suture and with tightening of each throw one can see the air leak becoming minimal.
The anastomosis is secured using extracorporeal knots.
One can appreciate both the upper and the middle lobe now getting fully ventilated and this was also reiterated with no air leak on the mechanical ventilator.
The anus sutures are divided.
An intercostal tube is placed through the same uterine incision.
